content:
- Started choose-task flow.
- Read list-active-tasks skill, progress log, current task summary, and done-task summary.
- Current summary shows one active failing task so far, but I still need to refresh summaries and read chooser rules before selecting.

- Refreshed Ralph task summaries and read choose-task rules.
- Confirmed repo shape from codebase: Nix + Rust workspace under crates/, plus vendored Go code under cockroachdb_molt/molt and docs/investigations.
- Re-reading refreshed current task summary and greping for blockers, priorities, and meta-task state before selecting.

- Refreshed current and done task summaries after reading choose-task rules.
- Confirmed there is exactly one active candidate in .ralph/current_tasks.md: story-34 docs rewrite with passes=false, no visible blocked_by tag, and no active failing bug outranking it.
- Next action is to write that task path to .ralph/current_task.txt and stop.

- Wrote .ralph/current_task.txt with the selected task path.
- Verified the selector result points to story-34 docs rewrite task.
- If resumed, the next agent should execute .ralph/tasks/story-34-human-friendly-docs-rewrite/01-task-rewrite-docs-for-humans-through-deepseek-opencode-only.md.

- Read previous Ralph progress, current task pointer, and the required tdd/improve-code-boundaries skill instructions.
- Confirmed the selected task is story-34 docs rewrite under .ralph/tasks/story-34-human-friendly-docs-rewrite/.
- Next: read the task file, determine whether it is in planning or execution state, and follow the task protocol exactly.

- Read the selected story-34 docs rewrite task and confirmed it starts in planning state, not execution state.
- Noted the task-specific TDD exception: this is documentation work, so verification must be command/workflow based rather than Rust tests.
- Next: read the opencode skill and inspect Ralph plan conventions so I can create the required plan file with the right status marker and path.

- Confirmed story-34 is a docs-boundary rewrite over existing README plus docs/public_image_operator_guide, not a greenfield docs creation task.
- Found likely factual drift to target in execution: current README references ghcr.io/<owner>/cockroach-migrate-{runner,verify}, while the existing operator guide still references ghcr.io/djosh34/{runner-image,verify-image} and Quay mirrors.
- Next: capture available opencode-go model ids and CI image-publication facts, then write the story-34 plan file and task <plan> pointer with TO BE VERIFIED.

- Created the story-34 plan file at .ralph/tasks/story-34-human-friendly-docs-rewrite/01-task-rewrite-docs-for-humans-through-deepseek-opencode-only_plans/2026-04-29-human-friendly-docs-rewrite-plan.md.
- Updated the story-34 task file to point at that plan via a <plan> tag.
- Plan records the available opencode model ids, the current docs boundary smells, the DeepSeek-only authorship workflow, the critic gate, and ends in TO BE VERIFIED. Next turn should review the plan, switch to NOW EXECUTE only if still sound, and then stop immediately per the task protocol.

- Re-read the story-34 task file, the saved plan, and the improve-code-boundaries smell guidance before deciding whether the plan was ready.
- Confirmed the plan already covers the required execution rules: DeepSeek V4 Pro is the only documentation prose author, README vs canonical deep-doc boundary is explicit, required critic models are named, and validation includes make check/make lint/make test without test-long.
- Switched the plan terminator from TO BE VERIFIED to NOW EXECUTE at .ralph/tasks/story-34-human-friendly-docs-rewrite/01-task-rewrite-docs-for-humans-through-deepseek-opencode-only_plans/2026-04-29-human-friendly-docs-rewrite-plan.md.
- Next turn should execute that plan exactly, starting with factual audit artifacts and opencode model use, and must stop/revert to TO BE VERIFIED immediately if the doc boundary or model availability proves wrong.

- Read previous Ralph progress, current task pointer, task file, execution plan, improve-code-boundaries smell references, and opencode skill.
- Confirmed the task is in NOW EXECUTE and that DeepSeek V4 Pro via opencode-go must be the sole documentation prose author; Codex may only gather facts, verify, and transcribe model-authored docs verbatim.
- Next: verify exact opencode-go model ids at execution time, audit authoritative docs/code/image sources into story artifacts, then run the DeepSeek rewrite pass.

- Verified required opencode-go models at execution time: deepseek-v4-pro, glm-5.1, kimi-k2.6.
- Audited authoritative sources for image publication, runner CLI/config/endpoints, verify-service CLI/config/API, and SQL generator inputs.
- Confirmed the main factual contradiction to fix in the rewrite: README uses ghcr.io/<owner>/cockroach-migrate-{runner,verify}:<sha> while CI/workflow/scripts publish runner-image and verify-image; created story-local audit and DeepSeek prompt artifacts under .ralph/tasks/story-34-human-friendly-docs-rewrite/artifacts/.
- Next: run DeepSeek V4 Pro through opencode with the prompt artifact, capture its verbatim markdown manifest, then transcribe exactly what it authors.

- DeepSeek-authored docs landed in the workspace as README.md plus a new canonical docs/operator-guide/ tree.
- Verified the new docs against repo facts and found two concrete issues: runner compose flag order was wrong, and verify-service walkthrough mixed HTTP startup with HTTPS/mTLS curl examples.
- Sent those findings back through opencode; DeepSeek corrected runner.md and verify-service.md in place. Also confirmed the old public_image_operator_guide/setup_sql/tls docs were already removed, then manually deleted the remaining duplicate scripts/README.md to keep one canonical human-facing docs surface.
- Next: run final factual scan, then the required harsh critic gate with DeepSeek, GLM 5.1, and Kimi 2.6, then make check/lint/test.

- Reworked docs extensively via DeepSeek opencode passes: canonical docs/operator-guide tree, README routing table, getting-started, architecture, config-reference, TLS/dev-cert guidance, health-check guidance, and multiple factual corrections.
- `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` are all green on the current state.
- Critic gate is still not fully green. Latest statuses:
  - GLM 5.1: PASS on the latest substantial structure.
  - DeepSeek V4 Pro: last PASS was before the most recent tiny Kimi-driven fixes; after that DeepSeek was not rerun.
  - Kimi 2.6: latest verdict is FAIL with five concrete concerns: getting-started vs installation order, installation entrypoint/default-command wording for runner-image, unclear TLS default when runner destination tls block is omitted, and a complaint that docs use Compose `configs:` which Kimi treats as swarm-only.
- Because the task requires all three critics to agree, do NOT mark passes=true, do NOT task-switch, and do NOT commit/push yet.
- Next turn should decide whether to keep iterating on Kimi's latest findings or challenge any of them against authoritative sources before another DeepSeek rewrite pass, then rerun DeepSeek/Kimi/GLM as needed.

- Read persisted Ralph progress log.
- Confirmed the active work is the story-34 docs rewrite task and prior state said make check/lint/test were green, but critic agreement was still incomplete.
- Next I need to re-read .ralph/current_task.txt, the task file, and its plan so I can resume from the exact required gate and avoid marking the task done too early.

- Re-read the task file, execution plan, and required tdd/improve-code-boundaries skill notes.
- Confirmed the task is in NOW EXECUTE and the repo already has substantial uncommitted documentation changes from prior DeepSeek-authored passes.
- Next I need to inspect the current docs/critic artifacts and rerun required validation on the exact working tree state before deciding whether the task can be marked complete.

- Read current docs and critic artifacts, then compared the live docs against source-of-truth code and OpenAPI.
- Confirmed several critic findings were stale: verify-service health checks are already documented via /metrics, architecture already covers failure modes, TLS page already includes dev self-signed certificate generation, and config-reference already has field types/defaults.
- Verified remaining live issues for the next DeepSeek pass: README routing/ownership is still too busy, installation leaks internal CI details and uses ./config instead of $(pwd)/config, and the docs boundary still repeats some installation/TLS ownership.

- Ran a targeted DeepSeek repair pass against the live docs bundle and mechanically applied only the model-authored rewrites for README.md, docs/operator-guide/installation.md, and docs/operator-guide/config-reference.md.
- Rebuilt the live docs bundle and reran the full final critic gate with opencode-go/deepseek-v4-pro, opencode-go/glm-5.1, and opencode-go/kimi-k2.6. All three now return VERDICT: PASS with no findings.
- Next I need to run make check, make lint, and make test on the exact working tree, then if they all pass I can mark passes=true, task-switch, commit, and push.
